content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

MARIN COUNTY
591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000
MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

February 6, 1991

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard, Chief
Technical Advice and Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, 6th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Jeanne:

On behalf of the California Legislative Forum ("CLF"),
this to request confirmation of telephone advice you gave me on
January 23, 1991 relative to the application of the gift limits
of Government Code sections 89504 and 89505.

The facts as I outlined then to you are the same as in
my letter of January 15, 1990 requesting advice. (You addressed
FPPC Advice Letter No. A-90-057 to me on behalf of CLF last
year.) Copies of those letters are attached. CLF is an
organization established to foster better relationships between
state and federal officials. Its primary activity is to host a
one-time annual event in Washington D.C., centered around a
dinner to which state and federal officials are invited.

With respect to Government Code sections 89504 and
89505, which limit gifts to certain state public officials to
$250 per calendar year from a single source, you advised me that:

(1) CLF would be a "single source" and subject to the
$250 per calendar year gift limit to each such
public official;

(2) For "activity expense" reporting by sponsors of
CLF's events, each sponsor would report its
payment to CLF as its "Total Amount of Activity"
and show the full amount of the value of the CLF
gift to each public official as the "Name and
Official Position of Reportable Persons and Amount
Benefitting Each" on its Lobbyist-Employer report;
and

(3) For determining the amount of the "gift" to each
public official for purposes of Government Code
section 89504 and 89505 limits, each sponsor would
deduct the value of meals and other benefits
received by its employees or agents who attend the
CLF event from its total sponsor payments, and
then calculate the gift per public official by
dividing the remainder amount by the number of

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard, Chief
February 6, 1991
Page 2

public officials and others who received benefits
(meals, etc.) for which there was no consideration
provided.

Assume the following: a sponsor paid CLF $2,000; the
sponsor sent two employees to attend and participate in CLF
events; the per capita value of meals, etc. for each person who
attended was $200.00; 50 persons (California public officials and
others) received meals, etc. without providing consideration; and
the sponsor was not affiliated with CLF under Regulation 18531.5,
which defines "single source" for gift purposes.

You advised that the "gift" by each $2,000 sponsor to
each such California public official would be calculated as
follows:

(a)  $200 each would be shown as the "activity
     expense" per reportable public official on
     each sponsor's Lobbyist-Employer report. It
     is permissible to footnote that entry to
     explain that the filer's portion of the gift
     is $32.00, as discussed below.

(b)  The section 89504/89505 "gift" to each
     official would be:

          $2,000.00    Sponsor's payment
          -  400.00    Sponsor's employees' benefits
          __________
          1,600.00
          ÷     50     Persons who received benefits
                       without consideration (exclu-
                       ding sponsor's employees)
          __________

          $   32.00    Amount of gift per public
                       official

This $32.00 would apply toward the sponsor's $250 per calendar
year limit to each participating public official.

Please confirm this advice to me as soon as possible.
I appreciate your consideration of this matter; if you have any
questions, please contact me.

Very truly yours,

CHARLES H. BELL, JR.

CHB:kab
6523.01
Attachments
